name: PixVerse
tagline: AI video generation with cinematic camera control and native audio
description:
PixVerse V6 generates 15-second 1080p videos from a single text prompt - including camera movement, lighting, and synchronized audio in one shot. Where most AI video tools still require separate steps for visuals and sound, PixVerse does both at once. Version 6 added over 20 cinematic lens controls (focal length, depth of field, chromatic aberration) and multi-shot storytelling, making it one of the more capable options for creators who want cinematic output without a production team.

The platform has 100 million users across 175 countries and an API that integrates with coding agents like Cursor and Claude Code - useful if you want to build video generation into your own workflows.

The main catch is credits. Free users get 90 credits upfront and 60 per day, but videos are watermarked and capped at lower resolution. Paid plans start at $8/month, but a single polished clip can eat through credits fast if you need multiple attempts to get it right.
features: 20+ cinematic lens controls (focal length, aperture, depth of field)
API and CLI for developer/agentic workflows
Compatible with Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex
pros: Audio and video generated simultaneously - no separate step
Cinematic camera controls most competitors lack
Strong character consistency across multi-shot scenes
Free tier available with daily credit refresh
cons: Credits don't roll over month to month
Multiple attempts per clip eats credits fast
Free tier has watermarks and resolution limits
